scope: Namecoin child coinbase payout address -> pool identity registry for merge-mined child-miner attribution.

source.name: project-maintained namecoin child-payout registry
source.notes: Maps a child-chain coinbase payout/reward address to a project-stable pool slug, derived from authenticated AuxPoW linkage: each address consistently co-occurs with one pool's independently-attributed Bitcoin parent coinbase across many merge-mined blocks. Pools that rotate their child payout address per block (notably ViaBTC on Namecoin/Syscoin) cannot be pinned to a single address and are intentionally omitted. Shared/protocol/relay outputs that appear across many pools are excluded.

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 637 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'antpool': 594, 'braiins-pool': 41, 'redrock-pool': 2} (purity 0.932).
evidence.note: operator hub: Braiins (42/42) and RedRock route 100% of their Namecoin rewards to this AntPool wallet (they run no Namecoin merge-mining of their own), so the minority BTC parents are a proxy relationship, not a conflict; see docs/attribution.md

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 383 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'f2pool': 383} (purity 1.000).
10 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'poolin': 10} (purity 1.000).
117 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'secpool': 117} (purity 1.000).
132 active merge-mined blocks carry this address; their independently BTC-coinbase-attributed parents reconcile to {'spiderpool': 132} (purity 1.000).
